root_motivation: The quarterly zine's bottleneck is the moment-extractor step — without a CLI that walks session JSONLs and surfaces ~50 ranked candidate excerpts, the editor (jsy) has to read every transcript by hand to find moments worth printing. Phase 0 ships only the extractor; layout/print/mail are explicitly downstream and human-driven.
user_persona: jsy at quarter-end, running the extractor against the last 90 days of Claude Code session JSONLs (~/.claude/projects/-home-jsy/*.jsonl), reading the ranked candidates in terminal, and using the JSON to seed manual selection. Audience for the JSON: jsy reading it before manual curation, no downstream automation in Phase 0.

unfakeable_metric.name: acceptance_tests_passing
unfakeable_metric.harness_command: cargo test --tests 2>&1 | grep -E 'test result: ok' | wc -l

acceptance_criteria.description: `zine extract --since <duration> [--root <jsonl-dir>] [--limit <N>] [--format json|text]` walks all *.jsonl files modified within the duration (e.g. 90d, 30d), extracts conversation turns, scores each turn pair (user + assistant) with the interest heuristic, and prints the top N (default 50) as JSON (default) or human-readable text.

JSON output shape: top-level `{since, generated_at, root, total_turns_scanned, returned, moments: [...]}`. Each moment: `{session_id, turn_index, ts, user_text, assistant_text, score, why}`. `why` is a one-sentence string explaining the score signals that fired.

Interest heuristic is a weighted sum: (a) length bell curve peaking ~600 chars total, (b) user-redirect keywords in turn N+1 (`wait`, `no actually`, `stop`, `hmm`) — indicates surprise, (c) code blocks in the assistant text (positive weight), (d) novelty score: ratio of words new to this session. Each component's contribution appears in the `why` string.
Default --root is `~/.claude/projects/-home-jsy/` (the Claude Code project transcripts directory). Non-existent root → exit 2 with stderr containing the path and `no jsonl files found`.

Turn pairs are extracted from the JSONL by walking records and pairing a `user` role record with the next `assistant` role record. Records that don't fit the pair (system reminders, tool calls in isolation) are skipped, not paired.
`--exclude-tool-only` (default true) drops assistant turns whose text content is empty (tool-only invocations). `--include-tool-only` includes them.

`--format text` prints each moment as: a horizontal rule, the session_id/turn_index/timestamp/score header, blockquoted user_text, blank line, assistant_text, blank line, `(why: <reason>)` italicised. Easy to skim in less.

`--errors-only` filter selects only turn pairs where the assistant's NEXT turn (N+2) contains apology keywords (`sorry`, `my mistake`, `you're right`). Mirrors the zine's mandatory `Errors and Apologies` section.

scope: Walk JSONL files under a root directory (default ~/.claude/projects/-home-jsy/)
Score each pair with weighted-sum interest heuristic (length + redirect-detection + code-density + novelty)
non_goals: Anonymization of third parties (editor's manual responsibility)
LLM-based reranking (heuristic only; no model calls)

five_whys_trace.q: Why ship the extractor before layout/print integration?
five_whys_trace.a: Because the heuristic is the only part that's automatable AND that needs taste-iteration — and you can't iterate the heuristic without running it against real transcripts and reading the outputs. Layout/print are taste-laden but manual; they don't benefit from being scripted in Phase 0.

five_whys_trace.q: Why a heuristic rather than asking the LLM what's interesting?
five_whys_trace.a: Because the heuristic runs across hundreds of MB of transcripts cheaply and reproducibly, and the editor still picks the final ~12 manually. An LLM rerank would be slow + expensive + non-reproducible — wrong tool for the surfacing stage.

five_whys_trace.q: Why include `why` (signal attribution) in the output?
five_whys_trace.a: Because the heuristic will be wrong sometimes — and when the editor disagrees with the score, they need to know WHICH signal caused it so they can mentally discount it (or file a bug for the weight tuning).
